title: Podman containers do not reach certain hosts
body:
### Issue Description

Requests to some hosts from within a podman container seem to time out when not using --network=host.

This applies to e.g. https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory and several other hosts, but not to e.g. https://www.google.com

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I have the problem that the following HTTP request in a container on rootless podman and rootful podman times out:

```bash
podman run --rm docker.io/curlimages/curl:latest curl --verbose https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
```

When using the host network everything works fine:

```bash
podman run --rm --network=host docker.io/curlimages/curl:latest curl --verbose https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
```

### Describe the results you received

```bash
podman run --rm docker.io/curlimages/curl:latest curl --verbose https://acme-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Podman rootless user was created with:
```bash
user="xyz"
sudo useradd --system --add-subids-for-system --create-home --home-dir "/var/lib/${user}" ${user}
sudo loginctl enable-linger ${user}
```

A reset with podman system prune was already done - No change noticed.

Podman was also uninstalled with `sudo pacman -Rns podman` and reinstalled with `sudo pacman -S podman`.

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
I can confirm the issue, it also happens with package registries such as registry.npmjs.org or registry.yarnpkg.com. I've also experienced issues with nuget.

I was able to find a workaround by downgrading passt to passt-2025_09_19.623dbf6. this solves the issue.

I can confirm this issue as well on **CachyOS** (Arch Linux based).

In my case, the issue was even more severe: **no internet access at all** from my containers. Nothing was responding, all requests were timing out.

**Workaround confirmed:** Downgrading to `passt-2025_09_19.623dbf6` as suggested by @devlux resolved the issue for me.

Same problem here, seems like it's limited to https-requests / openssl (examples: npm, updating certain ppa's with apt update within the container).

(downgrading passt works around the issue)

I ran into this issue as well. Very weird since some internet works (wget) in the container while others do not (alpine apk). I am not proficient enough to understand what is going on.

FIXED by downgrading to passt-2025_09_19.623dbf6

`podman run -it --replace --pull=newer --name=alpine docker.io/library/alpine:latest apk update`
Downgrading to passt-2025_09_19.623dbf6 also resolved all the strange network issues I was having recently. Was having with issues w/ plex, PIA among others.

> Seems to be caused by: https://bugs.passt.top/show_bug.cgi?id=183

indeed, for me the bugfix `passt-2025_12_15.b40f5cd` solves the networking issues with the package registries behind cloudflare.

I literally installed CachyOS to use primarily as a podman host a week ago, and I've been pulling my hair out with this issue all week. Weirdly enough, when the container is using the host network, I do not have connection issues. Only when they are being added to a bridge network. I will keep an eye on this thread.

> I can confirm this issue as well on **CachyOS** (Arch Linux based).
> 
> In my case, the issue was even more severe: **no internet access at all** from my containers. Nothing was responding, all requests were timing out.
> 
> **Workaround confirmed:** Downgrading to `passt-2025_09_19.623dbf6` as suggested by [@devlux](https://github.com/devlux) resolved the issue for me.

> I literally installed CachyOS to use primarily as a podman host a week ago, and I've been pulling my hair out with this issue all week. Weirdly enough, when the container is using the host network, I do not have connection issues. Only when they are being added to a bridge network. I will keep an eye on this thread.
> 
> > I can confirm this issue as well on **CachyOS** (Arch Linux based).
> > In my case, the issue was even more severe: **no internet access at all** from my containers. Nothing was responding, all requests were timing out.
> > **Workaround confirmed:** Downgrading to `passt-2025_09_19.623dbf6` as suggested by [@devlux](https://github.com/devlux) resolved the issue for me.

Confirmed! The downgrade fixed the networking issue in my containers experiencing this issue. Thank you so much @devlux !

Maybe this should be closed as the issue is with passt instead of podman, and a fix already released?

(after upgrading to passt-2025_12_15.b40f5cd-1, I also have no issues).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
tested the reproducer and looks fixed to me
